agent.name: runtime-bug-hunter
agent.role: Finds runtime and logic defects that can cause incorrect behavior or missed processing.

findings.title: Pull request events use hardcoded fallback changed files, causing false-positive audit enqueues
findings.description: For pull_request payloads, `payload.commits` is typically absent, so the code falls back to hardcoded file paths (`src/components/Button.tsx`, `src/utils/api.ts`). This causes `determineAuditSuites` to run on synthetic files and enqueue audits even when the PR does not touch qualifying files.

proof_hooks.summary: Fallback hardcoded files are used when `payload.commits` is falsy.
GitHub `pull_request` webhook payloads do not provide commit-level `added/modified` arrays on `payload.commits`; changed files must be fetched separately (e.g., PR files API) or derived differently.

findings.impact: Unnecessary audit jobs are queued, increasing compute cost and queue noise, and can trigger audits unrelated to actual PR diffs.

suggested_fix.approach: Do not use hardcoded fallback files in production flow. For pull_request events, fetch actual changed files from GitHub API (or skip with explicit reason if unavailable).
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Requires authenticated GitHub API call path and pagination handling for large PRs.
tests_needed: Unit: pull_request with no `payload.commits` does not enqueue unless real changed files are resolved.
Integration: PR with docs-only changes results in zero enqueued suites.

history.notes: New finding in current re-audit; not present as open duplicate in open_findings.json.

findings.title: Push change extraction ignores removed files, missing audits for deletion-only commits
findings.description: The push-event changed-file list includes only `added` and `modified`, omitting `removed`. If a commit only deletes logic-relevant files, suite routing can incorrectly skip audits.

proof_hooks.summary: Changed files are built from `added` and `modified` arrays only.
GitHub push commit entries include `removed`; deletion-only changes are possible and currently unaccounted for.

findings.impact: Deletion-only changes can bypass intended audit coverage, reducing detection of regressions introduced by file removals.

suggested_fix.approach: Include `c.removed` in the flattened changed-file list for push events.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Very low risk; only expands routing input set.
tests_needed: Unit: push payload with removed-only `.ts` file routes to logic suite.
Unit: mixed added/modified/removed behavior remains stable.

history.notes: New finding in current re-audit; not present as open duplicate in open_findings.json.

next_actions.action: Replace pull_request hardcoded fallback with real file resolution (GitHub PR files API) or explicit no-op
next_actions.rationale: Prevents synthetic diffs from triggering incorrect audit suite routing.

next_actions.action: Include `removed` paths in push changed-file extraction
next_actions.rationale: Restores coverage for deletion-only commits and avoids missed audits.

next_actions.action: Add webhook routing tests for pull_request-without-commits and removed-only push payloads
next_actions.rationale: Locks in behavior and prevents regressions in event payload handling.
